title: Fix the cost model: collection steps -> bit operations (binary collection)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The complexity must ultimately be stated in **bit operations**, because exponents are
arbitrary integers whose size grows during collection. The solution must fix a cost model and
justify it.

**Required.**
1. Fix the integer-arithmetic cost $M(B)$ for operands of bit-length $\le B$ (schoolbook $B^2$
   or $B\log B$) and state it.
2. Bound the cost of one collection step (one multiplication of a length-$\le n$ normal word by
   a relation RHS with $\le n$ entries) as $O(n\,M(B))$.
3. **Crucially**, collecting a power $g_i^{m}$ naively by $|m|$ single conjugations costs
   $\Theta(|m|)=\Theta(2^{b})$ — exponential in the exponent bit-length. Adopt Gebhardt's
   *symbolic/binary collection* (repeated squaring of the conjugation action) to reduce this to
   $O(b)=O(\log|m|)$ multiplications. Without this, the stated complexity is exponentially worse
   than necessary. State the binary-collection routine in an `algorithm` environment and prove
   its correctness, termination, and per-power cost.

**Deliverable.** Combine with the step-count and exponent-blow-up bounds to give the total
bit-complexity $T(\ell,n,b)$.
